In the 2Q FY2025 report for Stellantis, the Powertrain mix evolution reveals significant dynamics across various categories.
The Internal Combustion segment sold 202,000 units, generating revenue of €1.5B, holding a market share of 45%, with a
growth rate of 3%, classified as Stable within the Hybrid Powertrains context. The Hybrid category recorded 150,000 units sold,
amounting to €1.2B in revenue, representing 35% market share and a growth rate of 5%, categorized as Growing. The Electric
Vehicles segment showed sales of 120,000 units, with revenue of €1.0B, a market share of 15%, and 12% growth rate, labeled
as Increasing. The Total Powertrains sold amounted to 472,000 units, generating €3.7B in revenue, capturing 95% of the market
share, with no growth rate available. Looking ahead, for the Forecast 2025 in Q3 FY2025, the Internal Combustion segment is
projected to sell 210,000 units, generating €1.6B, with a market share of 44% and a growth rate of 2%, remaining Stable. The
Hybrid category is expected to sell 160,000 units for €1.3B, achieving a market share of 36% and a 6% growth rate, classified
as Growing. Electric Vehicles are forecasted to sell 130,000 units, generating €1.1B, with a market share of 16% and a growth
rate of 13%, categorized as Increasing. The Total Powertrains forecast for 500,000 units amounts to €4.0B in revenue, achieving
96% market share, with no growth rate available. For the Forecast 2026 in Q4 FY2025, the Internal Combustion segment is
anticipated to sell 220,000 units for €1.7B, with a 43% market share and a 1% growth rate, labeled as Stable. The Hybrid
segment is projected to sell 170,000 units, generating €1.4B, representing a 37% market share and 7% growth rate, categorized
as Growing. The Electric Vehicles segment is set to sell 140,000 units, generating €1.2B, with a 17% market share and a growth
rate of 14%, classified as Increasing. The Total Powertrains projection for 530,000 units will yield €4.3B in revenue, capturing
97% market share with no growth rate available. Looking further to the Forecast 2027, the Total Units, Projected Revenue,
Overall Market Share, Growth Prediction, and Future Status remain to be detailed, with Internal Combustion projected at
230,000 units for €1.8B and a 42% market share with 0% growth rate, classified as Stable, while the Hybrid category
anticipates selling 180,000 units and generating €1.5B.
